overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under California Civil Code Section 1671
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may not owe liquidated damages on termination

overrides.override_summary: Venue limitation to VA may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to litigate in California

overrides.override_summary: General release requirements subject to CA Franchise Investment Law (Corp Code 31512) and Franchise Relations Act (B&P Code 20010)
overrides.why_it_matters: Waivers of FIL/FRA rights are void

overrides.override_summary: NY choice of law may not be enforceable for claims under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law may apply to CA franchise relationships

overrides.override_summary: CA B&P Code 20000-20043 provides termination/non-renewal rights
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional protections beyond FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: General release does not relieve liability under Hawaii franchise laws
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot waive Hawaii Franchise Investment Law claims

overrides.override_summary: Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act governs for IL licensees. NY choice of law, venue, jury waiver cannot waive IL FDD Act rights
overrides.why_it_matters: IL law supersedes NY law for IL franchisees on franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Waivers of compliance with IL FDD Act are void (Section 41)
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot waive IL franchise law protections

overrides.override_summary: General release not required as condition of renewal/transfer except for claims not subject to Indiana Franchise Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchise law claims cannot be released as condition of transfer/renewal

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law may supersede FA on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional state protections on termination/non-renewal

overrides.override_summary: General release under 16.8 shall not release liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law. Release only for claims arising before transfer/renewal date.
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchise law claims protected

overrides.override_summary: May sue in Maryland for claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law. 3-year statute of limitations.
overrides.why_it_matters: Can litigate in MD for franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire or acknowledgment can waive claims under state franchise law or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong anti-waiver protection for MD franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Cannot terminate without good cause. 30-day cure required. Cannot refuse to renew without fair compensation for FMV of inventory/equipment (if term <5 years and post-term restrictions apply)
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong termination/non-renewal protections

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration or litigation outside Michigan is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Must arbitrate/litigate in Michigan

overrides.override_summary: Release/waiver depriving franchisee of Michigan act rights is void
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot waive Michigan franchise law protections

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse transfer except for good cause (reasonable qualifications, competitor, non-compliance, unpaid amounts)
overrides.why_it_matters: Transfer refusal grounds limited

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination with 60 days to cure; 180 days notice for non-renewal (Minn. Stat. 80C.14)
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice/cure periods than FA provides

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages provisions prohibited (Minn. Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: No liquidated damages payable in Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota
overrides.why_it_matters: Must litigate in Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: Jury trial waiver (Section 17.1) deleted entirely
overrides.why_it_matters: Right to jury trial preserved

overrides.override_summary: General release does not relieve liability under MN Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law claims protected

overrides.override_summary: Nothing in FDD or agreement can abrogate MN Statutes Chapter 80C rights
overrides.why_it_matters: MN law prevails on franchise law issues

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must indemnify franchisee against third-party trademark infringement claims (with notice/tender conditions)
overrides.why_it_matters: Unique MN protection — franchisor bears trademark defense costs

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law
overrides.why_it_matters: NY law may provide additional termination rights

overrides.override_summary: NY Franchises Law requires NY law govern NY franchise law claims. NY GBL Article 33 may supersede inconsistent FA provisions.
overrides.why_it_matters: NY law applies to NY franchise claims

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal/transfer may not be enforceable under NY law
overrides.why_it_matters: Release enforceability uncertain in NY

overrides.override_summary: All liquidated damages provisions deleted in their entirety
overrides.why_it_matters: No liquidated damages payable in ND

overrides.override_summary: ND law supersedes any conflicting FA provisions or NY law. FA governed by ND law.
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law controls entirely

overrides.override_summary: All venue/jurisdiction provisions designating forum outside ND are deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: Must litigate in ND

overrides.override_summary: Jury trial waiver deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: Right to jury trial preserved

overrides.override_summary: Release does not relieve liability under ND Franchise Disclosure Act
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law claims protected

overrides.override_summary: Release signed as condition of transfer/renewal does not apply to RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise law claims protected

overrides.override_summary: Venue restrictions outside RI void for claims under RI Franchise Investment Act. Foreign law application void for RI Act claims.
overrides.why_it_matters: Can litigate RI Act claims in RI under RI law

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause under VA Retail Franchising Act (Section 13.1-564). Unreasonable termination grounds may not be enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional termination protection for VA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: RCW 19.100.180 may supersede FA on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchise law may provide additional protections

overrides.override_summary: Release will not include WA Franchise Investment Protection Act rights except via negotiated settlement with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong protection — release requires independent counsel

overrides.override_summary: Restrictions on statute of limitations and jury trial rights under WA Act may not be enforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: Jury trial right may be preserved in WA

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in conflict of laws
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law controls on franchise matters

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees collectable only to extent reflecting reasonable estimated/actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: May limit $175K COO fee enforceability

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void unless employee earns >$100K/year or independent contractor earns >$250K/year (RCW 49.62). No-hire restrictions between franchisees of same franchisor void (RCW 49.62.060).
overrides.why_it_matters: Very strong noncompete and no-hire limitations in WA

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation site must be in WA or mutually agreed. Litigation for WA Act claims may be brought in WA.
overrides.why_it_matters: Can litigate/arbitrate in WA
